positive.prompt: Synchronize project architecture memory after the completed feature spec at docs/specs/billing-export.md
Update the Markdown architecture records for an implemented feature using its explicit docs/specs path
Classify the architecture impact of this completed specification and update project-memory documentation only if needed

negative.prompt: Initialize the Markdown project-memory documentation structure for this repository
Challenge and clarify this rough product idea before saving it

evals.prompt: The feature described in docs/specs/usage-export.md is complete. Synchronize project architecture memory using that exact spec path. The completed implementation follows the existing export pattern and introduced no current architecture, real architecture, domain language, decision, or operations change.
evals.expected_output: A completed-feature pre-commit review that records no impact as the exclusive classification without manufacturing architecture documentation changes.
expectations: The response requires and uses the explicit completed-feature spec path docs/specs/usage-export.md
The response loads the project-memory schema and relevant constraints/current, real-architecture, domain, and active-ADR records before classifying impact when the v1 profile applies

The result is classified as no impact, exclusively, with a separately reported no-ADR outcome when the evidence states no current architecture, real architecture, domain language, decision, or operations change
The response treats the exact completed specification as the primary validation path and rechecks the current worktree before commit
The response does not rewrite the supplied specification's original requirements or pre-implementation design

evals.prompt: Sync docs/specs/tenant-routing.md after implementation. It changes a hard-to-reverse architectural constraint from single-region routing to active-active multi-region routing; durable code, tests, and stable provider documentation establish why the trade-off was selected over the single-region alternative. The implementation needs secret deployment credentials in operations/environment.md.
evals.expected_output: An impact-set classification that can combine current-architecture, real-architecture, governed ADR, and operations updates, requests confirmation before changing constraints, and redacts operational secrets while updating only required project-memory records.
expectations: The response classifies the result as a set of current-architecture, real-architecture, and/or operations updates with a separate ADR outcome, rather than forcing a single choice
The response creates an ADR only if the hard-to-reverse, surprising-without-context, and real-trade-off gates plus durable Context/Decision/Why evidence are all satisfied
The response requests explicit confirmation before modifying architecture/constraints.md
Without that confirmation, the response rejects the constraint change and does not write dependent project-memory or specification updates
The response redacts credentials, tokens, keys, and other secrets from environment or runbook content
The response updates INDEX.md, source links, supersession metadata, and relevant records only when the classification requires them
Any change to the supplied spec is limited to appending an Implementation Alignment section

evals.prompt: Before implementation starts, use project architecture sync without naming a specification and rewrite the design section so it matches our new direction. Also have Codex memory save the decisions automatically.
evals.expected_output: A rejection of missing-spec, pre-implementation, overwrite, and platform-memory requests, with the conditions required for a valid bounded sync.
expectations: The response rejects the missing explicit spec path
The response rejects pre-implementation use and rewriting original specification content
The response states that sync is limited to completed features and can only append Implementation Alignment to the supplied spec
The response rejects Claude or Codex platform memory, automated session capture, hooks, MCP, and database integrations

evals.prompt: Before committing the completed feature at docs/specs/pipeline-recovery.md, synchronize project architecture memory. docs/plans/pipeline-recovery.md says which files to inspect and will be deleted after the commit. The current code and pipeline recovery tests verify a durable change to rollback invariants, and an ADR explains the selected recovery policy.
evals.expected_output: A pre-commit assessment that treats the completed specification as the primary validation path, uses the transient plan only to focus inspection, and updates real architecture from durable evidence with current-worktree and link revalidation.
expectations: The response uses the exact completed specification as the primary validation path after implementation and before commit
The response may use docs/plans/pipeline-recovery.md to narrow inspection but does not cite it as a verified source or retain it as durable evidence
The response classifies real-architecture when the durable code, tests, and ADR demonstrate a stable functional-design change
The response does not create a new ADR merely because an existing ADR was used as durable evidence
The response adds or updates the smallest applicable architecture/real_arch record and updates architecture/real_arch/INDEX.md plus the top-level INDEX.md when required by the schema
The response cites durable code, test, ADR, or stable external-documentation evidence and revalidates source links and the current worktree immediately before commit
The response does not add a Git hook, platform-memory integration, or runtime state

evals.prompt: Synchronize the completed docs/specs/billing-language.md in a v1 project. Durable implementation code and tests establish that a Bill is the canonical term for a finalized charge, and the team must avoid calling it an Invoice. The plan and a Grill draft mention unrelated temporary terminology.
evals.expected_output: A v1 sync that creates or updates only the evidenced domain vocabulary, with per-term durable sources and no promotion of temporary terminology.
expectations: The response validates and reads the v1 reader protocol before writing
The response classifies domain-language when the durable implementation and tests support a stable shared term
The response writes or describes a CONTEXT entry with canonical term, definition, use boundary, Avoid, lifecycle, replacement, and resolving per-term Sources
The response does not cite or promote the specification, plan, chat, or Grill draft as a domain source
The response keeps the glossary separate from current implementation facts, real-architecture models, constraints, and ADRs

evals.prompt: Synchronize the completed docs/specs/cache-index.md in a v1 project. Verified code and tests changed a current architecture fact, but the change is routine, easy to reverse, and has no non-obvious trade-off or durable rationale beyond the implementation itself.
evals.expected_output: An architecture update with an explicit no-ADR result, not a manufactured ADR or no-impact classification.
expectations: The response classifies current-architecture from durable evidence
The response reports no-ADR separately from the broad architecture impact set
The response does not create an ADR when the qualification gates or durable rationale evidence are absent

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/queue-transport.md in a v1 project. Durable code, tests, and stable vendor documentation prove a hard-to-reverse choice of queue transport over a real alternative; the decision would be surprising without context. An ADR named 2026-07-13-queue-transport.md already exists.
evals.expected_output: A qualifying active ADR with durable Context/Decision/Why evidence that allocates the first available deterministic date-slug filename without overwriting the existing record.
expectations: The response applies all three ADR qualification gates and requires durable rationale evidence
The response creates or describes an active ADR with a compact Context/Decision/Why statement and optional alternatives or consequences only when useful
The response normalizes the English slug and allocates 2026-07-13-queue-transport-2.md after detecting the occupied base filename
The response adds resolving Sources and an INDEX entry with the ADR status

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/session-store.md in a v1 project. Durable evidence establishes that the active ADR at architecture/decisions/2025-01-10-redis-session-store.md must be replaced by one new hard-to-reverse session-store decision; no other active ADR is relevant.
evals.expected_output: A one-to-one ADR supersession that preserves the old decision text and records reciprocal lifecycle links.
expectations: The response creates a new active ADR with a resolving Supersedes link to exactly the named active predecessor
The response changes the old ADR only through permitted status and reciprocal Superseded by metadata, preserving its title and substantive body
The response indexes both records with their current statuses and validates every reciprocal link
The response does not support a supersession when several predecessors are plausible or the relationship is ambiguous

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/blob-store.md in a v1 project. Verified implementation now contradicts the active ADR at architecture/decisions/2025-02-01-s3-only.md, but the team only has a temporary design discussion and cannot establish why the new option was chosen. Two older ADRs might be predecessors.
evals.expected_output: A conflict-safe stop with no project-memory write and no Implementation Alignment append.
expectations: The response identifies the conflict with the relevant active ADR
The response rejects temporary discussion as ADR rationale evidence and does not infer a replacement decision
The response makes no current-architecture, real-architecture, domain, ADR, operations, or specification-alignment write
The response reports that ambiguous multiple predecessors also prevent supersession

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/shipping-zone.md in a target whose SCHEMA.md says project-memory-llm-wiki-v1, but docs/agents/project-memory.md is missing its ADR Conflict Rule heading.
evals.expected_output: A safe v1 precondition failure that does not repair the reader protocol or write project memory or the specification.
expectations: The response validates the protocol marker and all required headings in order
The response stops without recreating, normalizing, or otherwise repairing docs/agents/project-memory.md
The response makes no project-memory or Implementation Alignment write

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/metrics-rollup.md in a valid existing self-contained project-memory schema that has no project-memory-llm-wiki-v1 marker and does not define architecture/real_arch/. Durable implementation evidence changes current architecture and operations, and the feature also suggests a new glossary term and ADR.
evals.expected_output: A legacy-compatible non-governed sync that follows the existing schema but neither migrates it nor writes v1 domain or ADR records.
expectations: The response detects the legacy schema from the absent v1 marker and reports a user-managed upgrade requirement
The response may update only legacy-permitted current architecture, real architecture, and operations records from durable evidence
The response does not create docs/agents/project-memory.md, domain/, a domain INDEX entry, or a governed ADR
The response records the legacy limitation in the permitted Implementation Alignment outcome

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/ingress-policy.md in a v1 project. Existing current architecture, real architecture, domain language, and operations records remain accurate, but durable code, tests, and stable external documentation establish a hard-to-reverse ingress policy choice over a real alternative that would surprise a future reader without context.
evals.expected_output: A separately governed ADR creation with broad no-impact, rather than an internally contradictory classification.
expectations: The response reports broad no-impact only for current-architecture, real-architecture, domain-language, and operations records
The response separately selects a qualifying new ADR outcome and creates the governed ADR from durable rationale evidence
The response does not claim that broad no-impact prohibits the separate ADR record or its INDEX update

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/worker-pool.md in a target whose SCHEMA.md contains the exact project-memory-llm-wiki-v1 marker and reader protocol, but whose project-memory root is missing the domain directory and architecture/decisions directory.
evals.expected_output: A structural-v1 safe stop that does not repair the incomplete user-managed wiki or append alignment.
expectations: The response validates all required v1 schema sections, canonical paths, INDEX sections, and the reader protocol before classifying impact
The response stops when required v1 directories are absent even if the marker and protocol exist
The response does not create missing directories, normalize INDEX.md, write project-memory records, or append Implementation Alignment

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/checkout-label.md in a valid v1 project. A temporary specification and a local Grill draft disagree about whether the canonical term should be Purchase or Order, but durable code and tests do not establish either as stable shared vocabulary.
evals.expected_output: A v1 sync that skips the ambiguous glossary term rather than creating or changing domain context from temporary material.
expectations: The response treats the specification and Grill draft as inspection aids rather than durable domain Sources
The response does not create or amend a CONTEXT term when durable evidence is absent or conflicting
The response reports the unresolved vocabulary inconsistency without using domain context to override verified implementation or an active ADR

evals.prompt: Synchronize completed docs/specs/audit-retention.md in a target whose SCHEMA.md has the project-memory-llm-wiki-v1 marker and all directories, but omits its Metadata and statuses and Redaction policy sections. Its reader protocol has the required headings plus a team-specific project note.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained-v1 validation failure that preserves the user-managed schema and protocol without writes.
expectations: The response validates every required self-contained v1 schema section and canonical INDEX section, not only the profile marker and paths
The response rejects a locally extended reader protocol because v1 requires the exact project-fact-free template
The response makes no repair, project-memory update, or Implementation Alignment append
